name: Edinburgh (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic gules of Edinburgh's arms

description: A white field bearing the coat of arms of the City of Edinburgh — a triple-towered castle on a rock between maiden and doe supporters, beneath the city's motto.
history: Edinburgh's coat of arms has been used since at least 1732 (Lord Lyon Court) and the modern flag was confirmed by the City of Edinburgh Council in 2007.

features.role: Civic coat of arms of Edinburgh
features.symbolism: a triple-towered castle on a rock between a maiden and a doe, with the city's motto 'Nisi Dominus Frustra' ('Without the Lord, [all is] in vain')
